question: Can a shadow-trained marginal distributional attack recover MIDST TabDDPM membership after nearest-neighbor distance was weak?

asset.source: MIDST SaTML 2025 black-box single-table starter-kit data

label_source.dev_final: local Codabench bundle challenge_label.csv files from the cloned MIDST repository

asset.task: black-box membership inference over diffusion-model-based synthetic tabular data

scorer.name: shadow_hist_gradient_boosting_marginal_distributional_features
scorer.evaluation_phase: dev and final labels used only for evaluation
scorer.features: per-row marginal z, absolute z, robust z, empirical CDF, tail-CDF, 90pct interval indicators, and row-level outlierness summaries relative to each model synthetic table
scorer.implementation_note: Executed as a one-off bounded scorer; no reusable tool was added because the result is weak and overfits shadow models.

decision.stop_reason: The scorer learns train shadow models strongly but does not transfer to dev/final. This closes MIDST marginal-distributional shadow learning as a next mainline mechanism; do not expand with classifier or feature-matrix sweeps.
